articles.title: ICT adoption can boost labour productivity by 76%, says SBI report
articles.description: An SBI report finds that a one-unit increase in ICT adoption can boost labour productivity by 76%. It also increases the likelihood of firm registration by an average of 84 percentage points, pushing businesses towards greater formalisation.

articles.title: 'Big Short' investor Michael Burry is not impressed with Anthropic valuation; says: Claude-maker's business of developing AI models is far too expensive, too much ...
articles.description: Michael Burry, known for predicting the 2008 housing crash, is questioning the high valuations of AI firm Anthropic and SpaceX. He believes hype, not fundamentals, is driving investor interest, particularly for Anthropic's AI model business, which he deems "far too expensive" and unsustainable long-term.

articles.title: US runs out of EB-2 visas for Indians this fiscal year, pauses fresh issuances until October
articles.description: The US has exhausted its annual allocation of EB-2 immigrant visas for Indian nationals, forcing a temporary halt to new issuances until October 2026. The move highlights the growing pressure on employment-based immigration pathways and could further lengthen waiting times for thousands of highly skilled Indian professionals.

articles.title: Microsoft AI CEO: My team is 'more concerned' about Anthropic, than Google, Meta and OpenAI
articles.description: Microsoft's AI chief, Mustafa Suleyman, revealed a strategic shift, prioritizing competition with Anthropic over rivals like Google and Meta. This focus stems from Anthropic's aggressive entry into enterprise software and coding tools, posing a direct threat to Microsoft's core business. Microsoft is now developing its own advanced AI models to counter this challenge and reduce reliance on OpenAI.

articles.title: Apple Vision Pro gains a free, native app that takes you on an immersive private flight
articles.description: There’s just something special about Apple Vision Pro and airplanes. From turning in-flight entertainment into a cinematic experience to supporting the world’s most advanced flight simulator, Apple Vision Pro has earned its wings. Now there’s a new native app that’s free to download and experience that takes Apple Vision Pro users on an immersive private flight from the comfort of your living room. more...

articles.title: Gita Gopinath: AI, business reforms to shield rupee from risks
articles.description: Gita Gopinath says India's policy focus should be on improving ease of doing business and building a positive AI narrative. These actions can attract capital, take pressure off the rupee, and act as a buffer against West Asia-related macro risks.

articles.title: IRCTC boosts security, deactivates 3 crore suspicious user IDs
articles.description: IRCTC has intensified its anti-fraud measures, deactivating 3.03 crore suspicious user IDs in 2025. The corporation also set new records, booking an average of 14.53 lakh tickets daily and achieving a peak of 37,410 tickets per minute.

articles.title: Tata Motors taps China's Chery for premium EV push: Report
articles.description: Tata Motors plans to license an automaking platform from China's Chery , four people familiar with the matter told Reuters, as the Indian car company seeks to get its delayed premium EVs back on track.

articles.title: UK MPs want Palantir out of NHS data platform, cite risks of overreliance on US tech giant
articles.description: Palantir has become a key technology partner for the NHS, defence and policing agencies across the UK. But a parliamentary committee now warns that the country's growing dependence on the US software company could create strategic vulnerabilities, prompting calls for ministers to seek a British alternative.

articles.title: Man gives up smartphone for a month, shares his experience
articles.description: A man has ditched his smartphone for a flip phone, finding most people deeply addicted to their devices. He discovered giving up the smartphone was easier than anticipated, leading to increased phone calls and a more present, thoughtful existence. He now prioritizes tangible items and has no plans to return to his smartphone.

articles.title: Made A Mistake In WhatsApp Status? You May Soon Be Able To Edit It
articles.description: WhatsApp is reportedly developing a new feature that would allow users to edit captions on their Status updates after posting. Currently, any errors necessitate deleting and re-uploading the entire Status.

articles.title: New Aadhaar app 2026: How to download, activate, and manage your digital ID
articles.description: UIDAI has introduced a brand-new Aadhaar mobile app with enhanced privacy controls, biometric security, selective data sharing, and family profile management. Follow this step-by-step guide to download and set up the app on your smartphone.
